title: Queenstown Family Deals
meta_desc: Find the best family deals in Queenstown, NZ. Bookme activity discounts, campervan hire, and more. Plan a fun and affordable family holiday in Queenstown, the.
intro: Queenstown is an ideal destination for families, offering a range of exciting activities and stunning scenery. With some smart planning, you can have a fantastic holiday without breaking the bank. Currently, you can find deals such as up to 40% off activities on Bookme.co.nz and family campervans from $89/day on Discover Cars.

sections.heading: Current Booking Deals and Discounts
sections.body:
There are several current deals that can help make your family holiday in Queenstown more affordable. Bookme.co.nz is offering up to 40% off activities, including the popular Skyline Gondola & Luge and Ziptrek Ecotours. Discover Cars has family campervans available from $89/day, which can be a cost-effective way to explore the area.

Additionally, Booking.com is offering free cancellation on most holiday park and motel bookings, providing you with more flexibility when planning your trip. Cover-More is also offering comprehensive family travel insurance from $4.50/day, giving you peace of mind while you're away.
list: Up to 40% off activities on Bookme.co.nz
Family campervans from $89/day on Discover Cars
Free cancellation on most bookings on Booking.com
Comprehensive family travel insurance from $4.50/day on Cover-More

sections.heading: Best-Value Activities
sections.body:
Some of the best-value activities in Queenstown include the Skyline Gondola & Luge, which costs around $40-$60 per person, and Ziptrek Ecotours, which costs around $150-$200 per person. The Queenstown Ice Arena is another great option, with prices from $20-$30 per person.

For something a bit different, consider taking a ride on the Kjet or Dart River Jet, which costs around $120-$150 per person. The TSS Earnslaw Steamship is also a great option, with prices from $80-$120 per person.

sections.heading: Money-Saving Tips for Families
sections.body: Here are some tips to help you save money on your family holiday in Queenstown:
list: Book your activities and accommodation in advance to get the best prices
Consider visiting during the shoulder season (April-May or September-October) when prices are lower
Look for packages that include activities and accommodation, which can often be cheaper than booking separately
Take advantage of free activities such as the Queenstown Gardens and frisbee golf, and the Lake Wakatipu foreshore walk
Pack a picnic lunch instead of buying food at restaurants or cafes
Use public transport or walk/bike whenever possible to save on transport costs

sections.heading: When to Book for Best Prices
sections.body:
It's a good idea to book your activities and accommodation in advance to get the best prices. For popular activities like the Skyline Gondola & Luge and Ziptrek Ecotours, it's recommended to book at least a week in advance. For accommodation, it's best to book at least a month in advance to get the best prices.

Additionally, consider booking during the shoulder season (April-May or September-October) when prices are lower. You can also use Bookme.co.nz to find deals and discounts on activities, and Discover Cars to find affordable campervan hire.
